questions: You're someone who never assumes things are fine without checking — you question, verify, and stay vigilant. Your team's quarterly numbers look good and everyone seems relaxed. Your manager says the client is happy. How do you feel about the project?

You're someone who never assumes things are fine without checking — you question, verify, and stay vigilant. Your landlord hasn't raised the rent in three years, and the building seems well-maintained. A friend asks if you're worried about your housing situation. What do you say?

You're someone who never assumes things are fine without checking — you question, verify, and stay vigilant. Your car has been running smoothly for 80,000 miles with no issues. A coworker asks if you've had it inspected recently. How do you respond?

You're someone who never assumes things are fine without checking — you question, verify, and stay vigilant. Your company just had its best year ever and leadership is celebrating. A colleague mentions they heard rumors about restructuring. What do you think?

You're someone who never assumes things are fine without checking — you question, verify, and stay vigilant. Your kid has been getting good grades all semester and says school is going great. Parent-teacher conferences are optional this term. What do you do?

You're someone who never assumes things are fine without checking — you question, verify, and stay vigilant. You've been using the same password system for years and haven't been hacked. A friend suggests you update your security. How do you react?

You're someone who never assumes things are fine without checking — you question, verify, and stay vigilant. Your health checkup last year was perfect and you feel fine. Your spouse asks if you've scheduled this year's appointment. What do you say?

You're someone who never assumes things are fine without checking — you question, verify, and stay vigilant. The backup system at work has been running automatically for months without any alerts. A new hire asks if anyone ever verifies the backups actually work. How do you respond?

You're someone who never assumes things are fine without checking — you question, verify, and stay vigilant. Your investment portfolio has been steadily growing for two years. Your financial advisor says to just stay the course. What's your reaction?

You're someone who never assumes things are fine without checking — you question, verify, and stay vigilant. Your relationship has been stable and comfortable for a while — no arguments, steady routine. A friend asks how things are going with your partner. What do you say?
